name: Poland (Rzeczpospolita Polska)
summary: Poland is a civil-law jurisdiction whose family courts (sąd rejonowy — wydział rodzinny i nieletnich) operate under the Kodeks rodzinny i opiekuńczy (KRO) of 1964 and the Kodeks postępowania cywilnego (KPC). The statutory framework is silent on the 'alienacja rodzicielska' label as a diagnostic category. The Sąd Najwyższy has not endorsed PAS as a syndrome but applies dobro dziecka (welfare-of-the-child) standard under KRO art. 56. The Rzecznik Praw Dziecka (Children's Rights Ombudsperson) and the Komitet Ochrony Praw Dziecka have expressed institutional caution toward PAS-syndromic framing. Poland's 2020 attempted withdrawal from the Istanbul Convention (subsequently retained under Tusk Government from December 2023) reflects political-context tension affecting DV-protection framework intersection with custody disputes. The Hague Abduction Convention (ratified 1992) operates with EU 2019/1111 Brussels IIb regulation framework.

statutory_framework.title: Family and Guardianship Code 1964
statutory_framework.relevance: Primary statutory basis for Polish family law — substantively foundational 1964 code amended multiple times including 2015 reform. Establishes władza rodzicielska (parental authority) framework, dobro dziecka standard, and post-divorce custody framework.

statutory_framework.title: Code of Civil Procedure
statutory_framework.relevance: Procedural code for civil proceedings including family-law matters. Substantively significant procedural framework for custody disputes and contact-refusal cases.

statutory_framework.citation: Ustawa o przeciwdziałaniu przemocy domowej (2005, amended 2023)
statutory_framework.title: Law on Counteracting Domestic Violence
statutory_framework.relevance: DV-protection framework establishing Niebieska Karta procedure and 2023 amendment introducing stronger DV-protection mechanisms — substantively significant for DV-PA-allegation intersection cases.

statutory_framework.citation: Hague Convention (Polish ratification + EU Regulation 2019/1111 Brussels IIb)
statutory_framework.title: International abduction + EU coordination framework
statutory_framework.relevance: Poland ratified 1980 Hague Convention 1 November 1992. EU Regulation 2019/1111 Brussels IIb effective 1 August 2022 coordinating cross-border family-law matters. Substantively significant international-abduction framework.

statutory_framework.title: Council of Europe Convention on preventing and combating violence against women and domestic violence
statutory_framework.relevance: Poland ratified Council of Europe Istanbul Convention 27 April 2015, in force 1 August 2015. 2020 PiS Government withdrawal attempt subsequently halted; Convention retained under Tusk Government from December 2023. Art. 31 requires contact/custody decisions take account of violence.

apex_courts.name: Sąd Najwyższy (Supreme Court of Poland)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil matters including family law; reviews appellate-court decisions via kasacja

apex_courts.name: Trybunał Konstytucyjny (Constitutional Tribunal)
apex_courts.role: Constitutional review including family-law statutory constitutionality

apex_courts.name: Sądy rejonowe — wydziały rodzinne i nieletnich
apex_courts.role: Specialised first-instance family courts within district courts

key_developments.title: Kodeks rodzinny i opiekuńczy 1964 — current family-code foundation
key_developments.description: Kodeks rodzinny i opiekuńczy promulgated 25 February 1964 (effective 1 January 1965) establishing socialist-era family-law framework. Substantively foundational current Polish family-law code surviving 1989 democratic transition through subsequent amendments.

key_developments.title: Poland ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: Poland ratified UN CRC 7 July 1991 (in force 7 July 1991). Substantively significant international child-rights framework — Sąd Najwyższy has applied dobro dziecka standard with UN CRC influence in subsequent jurisprudence.

key_developments.title: Poland ratifies Hague Abduction Convention
key_developments.description: Poland ratified 1980 Hague Convention on the Civil Aspects of International Child Abduction 1 November 1992 (in force 1 November 1992). Substantively significant international-abduction framework — subsequently coordinated with EU Brussels II framework.

key_developments.title: Konstytucja Rzeczypospolitej Polskiej 1997 — child-rights constitutional codification
key_developments.description: Konstytucja Rzeczypospolitej Polskiej adopted 2 April 1997 establishing art. 72 child-rights framework requiring state protection of child rights. Substantively significant post-democratization constitutional framework reinforcing UN CRC implementation.

key_developments.title: Poland EU accession + Brussels II framework integration
key_developments.description: Poland EU accession 1 May 2004 — operationally integrating Polish family-law framework with Council Regulation (EC) 2201/2003 Brussels IIa (subsequently Brussels IIb 2019/1111). Substantively significant cross-border family-law coordination framework.

key_developments.title: Ustawa o przeciwdziałaniu przemocy w rodzinie 2005
key_developments.description: Ustawa o przeciwdziałaniu przemocy w rodzinie 29 July 2005 establishing DV-protection framework with Niebieska Karta (Blue Card) procedure. Substantively foundational DV-protection statute — subsequently amended 2010, 2020, 2023.

key_developments.title: Poland ratifies Istanbul Convention + KRO reform
key_developments.description: Poland ratified Council of Europe Istanbul Convention 27 April 2015, in force 1 August 2015. Multiple KRO amendments 2015-2016 including reform of władza rodzicielska framework. Substantively significant family-law modernization reform package.

key_developments.title: PiS Government Istanbul Convention withdrawal attempt + Constitutional Tribunal abortion ruling
key_developments.description: 2020 PiS Government Istanbul Convention withdrawal attempt (subsequently halted) and Constitutional Tribunal abortion ruling 22 October 2020 reflecting political-context tension affecting reproductive-rights and DV-protection framework. Substantively significant political-context shift affecting family-law adjudication context.

key_developments.title: EU Regulation 2019/1111 (Brussels IIb) operational + Ukraine refugee framework
key_developments.description: EU Regulation 2019/1111 Brussels IIb effective 1 August 2022 coordinating cross-border family-law matters. Substantial Ukrainian refugee influx 2022+ operationally significant for Polish family-court jurisdiction over Ukrainian-national family-law matters. Substantively significant cross-border framework intersection.

key_developments.title: Ustawa o przeciwdziałaniu przemocy domowej 2023 amendment + Tusk Government Istanbul Convention retention
key_developments.description: Ustawa o przeciwdziałaniu przemocy domowej 2023 amendment effective 22 June 2023 introducing stronger DV-protection mechanisms. Tusk Government from December 2023 confirming Istanbul Convention retention reversing 2020 PiS withdrawal attempt. Substantively significant political-context shift restoring DV-protection framework trajectory.

key_developments.title: Konstytucyjny Trybunał crisis + KRO amendment framework
key_developments.description: Konstytucyjny Trybunał (Constitutional Tribunal) institutional crisis 2015-2022 affecting constitutional-review legitimacy regarding family-law statutes. Multiple KRO amendments 2018-2022 refining post-2015-reform parental-authority and visitation framework. Substantively significant institutional-context affecting subsequent family-court adjudication trajectory — establishing trajectory toward 2023 Tusk Government legitimacy-restoration framework and 2024 dobro dziecka substantive register.

key_developments.title: Sąd Najwyższy + Rzecznik Praw Dziecka jurisprudence — dobro dziecka substantive register
key_developments.description: Sąd Najwyższy and sąd rejonowy jurisprudence continue developing dobro dziecka interpretation under KRO + UN CRC + EU Brussels IIb framework. Rzecznik Praw Dziecka (Children's Rights Ombudsperson) institutional position critical of PAS-syndromic framing in custody-evaluation reports. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'alienacja rodzicielska' label adoption.

structural_findings: Poland operates a structurally distinctive civil-law family-law framework — KRO 1964 + Konstytucja 1997 art. 72 + EU Brussels IIb 2019/1111 framework — with substantive dobro dziecka analysis without doctrinal 'alienacja rodzicielska' label adoption.

2020 PiS Government Istanbul Convention withdrawal attempt and subsequent Tusk Government 2023 retention reflects political-context tension affecting DV-protection framework — substantively significant political-context data point for Central European family-law trajectory analysis.

Rzecznik Praw Dziecka (Children's Rights Ombudsperson) institutional position operates as substantively significant institutional-critique data point — placing Poland in caution-aligned cluster alongside European institutional-critique trajectory.
editorial_notes: Poland's KRO 1964 socialist-era family-code surviving 1989 democratic transition operationally creates structurally distinctive Central European civil-law family-law framework — substantively distinguishing Polish framework from Western European post-war codes (Germany BGB, France Code civil, Italy Codice civile 1942).

2020-2023 Istanbul Convention political-context tension is substantively significant for Central European DV-protection framework trajectory — making Poland a critical comparative-law data point for political-context-affected family-law adjudication.
